title: Simplify Boolean Expressions in Java
description: In Java, return of boolean expressions should not be wrapped in an 'if-then-else' statement. It can be simplified for better readability and efficiency. This rule helps to identify such scenarios where boolean expressions can be simplified.
recommendation: Simplify the boolean expressions by avoiding wrapping them into 'if-then-else' statements. For instance, instead of using 'if-then-else' to return boolean values, you can directly return the expression.
impact: Improves code readability and efficiency by simplifying boolean expressions.

noncompliant: boolean foo(Object param) { if (expression) { bar(param, true, "qix"); } else { bar(param, false, "qix"); } if (expression) { return true; } else { return false; } }
compliant: boolean foo(Object param) { bar(param, expression, "qix"); return expression; }

checks.message: Simplify the boolean expressions by avoiding wrapping them into 'if-then-else' statements.
